ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for green tea frappuccino; FOODON_03301710 (blended iced beverage) used as closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific code for blended iced green tea frappuccino exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for blended iced green tea frappuccino; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one medium cup of approximately 350 ml, equivalent to roughly 350 g, as commonly served at cafe chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Green Tea Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Green Tea Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream), CR ID D08010042, confidence 0.95. All per-100g values preserved from the input record which carries HPB provenance.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk-based sweetened beverages and blended tea drinks — analogous estimate based on sweetened dairy-tea beverage GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for green tea frappuccino (no whipped cream) in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low; GI ≤55 is classified low per University of Sydney scale) based on the closest analogous food category: sweetened milk-based blended beverages with moderate sugar content (~6.94g carbohydrate/100g). Sweetened dairy-tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database cluster in the 50–60 range. GI of 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low category (≤55) per the University of Sydney classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (55) × carbohydrate per serving (24.3g for 350g serving) / 100 = 13.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar syrup and sweetened beverage classification; low sodium (24 mg/100g) but SSB status raises cardiovascular metabolic risk
hypertension.guidance: Sodium content is low at 24 mg/100g, which is favourable for blood pressure; however, the added sugar syrup classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), and AHA 2018 guidelines recommend limiting SSBs for hypertensive patients due to sugar-driven metabolic and vascular risk — choose an unsweetened matcha version to reduce this concern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened blended beverage with added sugar syrup, zero dietary fibre, and glycemic index of 55
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage contains added sugar syrup and zero dietary fibre, qualifying it as an SSB under ADA guidelines; individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this drink and request an unsweetened matcha preparation with no syrup to minimise postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~1.1g/100g from milk base) and added sugar syrup; trans fat is 0g and sodium is low
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Total fat is modest at 1.76g/100g with an estimated saturated fat of ~1.1g/100g from the milk base; trans fat is 0g and sodium is low at 24 mg/100g, which are favourable; however, the added sugar syrup contributes to SSB classification, and AHA dietary guidelines recommend limiting SSBs for cardiovascular disease risk reduction — an unsweetened version is preferred.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Green tea catechins (EGCG) provide anti-inflammatory benefit; trans fat is 0g and saturated fat is low
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Matcha/green tea provides catechins and EGCG with evidence-based anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; trans fat is 0g and saturated fat is low (~1.1g/100g), so no RA hard gates are triggered, though the added sugar syrup contributes to inflammatory load and an unsweetened version is preferred.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Green Tea Frappuccino (No Whipped Cream) is a blended iced beverage made from green tea (matcha powder or concentrate), fresh or UHT milk, ice, and sugar syrup, served without whipped cream. The drink is smooth, creamy, and mildly sweet with a characteristic earthy, grassy matcha flavour and a light green colour. It is widely available at international cafe chains such as Starbucks and local cafes across Singapore and Southeast Asia, and is known in Chinese as 绿茶星冰乐（不加鲜奶油）and in Malay as minuman teh hijau campuran ais. Per 100g, it provides 49 kcal, 1.66g protein, 1.76g fat (saturated fat approximately 1.1g), 6.94g carbohydrate, 2.66g sugar, 0g dietary fibre, and 24mg sodium. Calcium is 55mg/100g from the milk base. The beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to the added sugar syrup, which is clinically relevant for type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease management. Green tea catechins (EGCG) provide anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low, at the upper boundary of the low category per University of Sydney scale) based on analogous sweetened dairy-tea beverages. A 350ml serving delivers approximately 13.4 glycemic load units. Individuals managing blood glucose, hypertension, or cardiovascular risk should request an unsweetened version with no syrup.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a green tea frappuccino without whipped cream?
Is green tea frappuccino suitable for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of a matcha frappuccino?
Green tea frappe no whip calories Singapore
Is green tea frappuccino high in sodium?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010042) with confidence 0.95 and reviewed provenance. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for sweetened dairy-tea beverages, documented transparently. Multilingual names provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to support Singapore-market retrieval.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this blended beverage is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (D08010042) as of 2026. cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database for this beverage entry. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per product formulation (no hydrogenated fats in standard green tea frappuccino without whipped cream). dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB source; green tea frappuccino without whipped cream contains no fibre-contributing ingredients in the blended preparation. potassium_mg set to 90.0: estimated from milk and green tea component contribution based on USDA FoodData Central reference values for similar milk-based beverages; not directly reported in HPB entry. saturated_fat_g set to 1.1: estimated from milk fat contribution within total fat of 1.76g/100g, consistent with standard low-fat milk base; not directly reported in HPB entry. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened dairy-tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement exists for this specific product. GI 55 is classified as low (≤55) per the University of Sydney GI classification scale. gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to align with University of Sydney scale where GI ≤55 = low. Multilingual local names added for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to support Singapore-market retrieval and satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance; this beverage is widely consumed in Singapore and multilingual coverage aids discoverability.
